words.tip: Sinaas (from China) + appel. Lit. 'China apple'! Plural: de sinaasappels.
Long 'ee'. Plural: de peren — open syllable keeps it long: pe-ren.
Aard (earth) + bei (berry). Plural: de aardbeien. 'Een bak aardbeien' = a punnet.
Long 'aa'. Plural: de bananen. 'Een tros bananen' = a bunch of bananas.
Plural: de kiwis — foreign word ending in vowel takes -'s? No — kiwi takes -s directly.
'ui' vowel. Plural: de druiven — f→v rule! 'Een tros druiven' = a bunch of grapes.
Long 'oo'. Plural: de noten. Diminutive: het nootje / nootjes.
Aard (earth) + appel. Lit. 'earth apple'! Plural: de aardappels.
Botanically a fruit — sold with vegetables in the shop! Plural: de tomaten.
No plural change. Italian origin. 'Een stuk broccoli' or just 'broccoli'.
Bloem (flower) + kool (cabbage). Note spelling: bloemkool, not bloomkool.
French origin, French pronunciation: [sham-pin-YON]. Plural: de champignons.
French origin. 'zh' = like 's' in 'measure'. Plural: de aubergines.
Belgian Dutch! NL Dutch uses 'knoflook'. 'Een teentje look' = a clove of garlic.
Plural: de mosselen (not 'mossels'!). Mosselen met frietjes = the Belgian classic.
